name: Socure ID+
what_it_does: AI-powered digital identity verification platform that uses predictive analytics, machine learning, and a consortium data network to verify identities, detect fraud, and automate KYC/AML compliance decisions in real time.
use_cases: Automate KYC identity verification during account opening by submitting PII and receiving a composite risk score with pass/fail decision in under 500ms
Screen new users against global watchlists, PEP lists, and adverse media as part of an AML compliance workflow triggered by account events
Verify government-issued document authenticity (driver's license, passport) using Docv module and associate it with a biometric selfie match
Route high-risk applicants to enhanced manual review queues based on Socure's fraud risk scores while auto-approving low-risk submissions
Trigger re-verification workflows when transaction monitoring surfaces suspicious activity on an existing account to refresh identity confidence
not_for: Business entity verification (KYB) — Socure ID+ is designed for individual consumer identity, not legal entities or corporate accounts
Self-serve or low-budget use cases — Socure requires enterprise contracts with significant minimum commitments not accessible to startups
International identity verification in markets where Socure's data consortium has limited coverage outside North America
best_when: Best when an agent must make real-time automated KYC or fraud pass/fail decisions on individual consumers at scale within a regulated financial services or fintech environment.
avoid_when: Avoid when verifying businesses instead of individuals, or when operating in a budget-constrained environment that cannot support enterprise contract minimums.

auth.notes: SDK token (API key) used for server-to-server calls. JWT tokens issued for short-lived document capture sessions in the Docv flow. Separate credentials per environment (sandbox, production).

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise contract with per-verification pricing and annual minimums; sandbox available post-contract
pricing.notes: No self-serve signup. Requires sales engagement and compliance review before access is granted. One of the higher-cost identity verification platforms in the market.

performance.rate_limits: High throughput tiers available by contract; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns structured JSON with reason codes and decision explanations; fraud module returns labeled signals that are useful for downstream agent routing logic.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Requests generate unique reference IDs; re-submitting identical PII does not return cached results — each call triggers a new verification and billing event.
known_agent_gotchas: Each API call is a billable event even in error cases; agents must implement guard logic before submitting to avoid runaway costs from retry loops
The Docv (document verification) module involves a front-end SDK capture flow that cannot be fully automated — requires a human to photograph their ID
Sandbox data is synthetic and does not exercise all fraud signal paths; production behavior on thin-file or manipulated inputs will differ from sandbox
Module selection (KYC, fraud, Docv, watchlist) must be specified per-request; forgetting a required module means resubmitting and paying again
Compliance requirements mandate that certain decision outcomes (e.g., adverse action) trigger regulatory obligations the agent orchestration layer must fulfill

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, ISO 27001 certified. Biometric and PII data handling subject to strict data retention and deletion SLAs. JWT tokens are short-lived with tight expiry.
